title: PHP Constants
content:
### PHP Constants

Constants are identifiers that hold a value that cannot change during script execution.

### Defining Constants

| Method | Syntax | Example |
|--------|--------|---------|
| `define()` | `define(name, value)` | `define("SITE", "example.com");` |
| `const` keyword | `const NAME = value` | `const VERSION = 1.0;` |

```php
<?php
define("PI", 3.14159);
define("SITE_NAME", "MyWebsite");
const MAX_USERS = 100;

echo PI;
echo SITE_NAME;
echo MAX_USERS;
?>
```

### Constant vs Variable

| Feature | Constant | Variable |
|---------|----------|----------|
| Prefix | None | `$` |
| Changeable | No | Yes |
| Global scope | Always | Depends on scope |
| Runtime definition | `define()` | Assignment |

> **Key Point:** Constants are automatically global across the entire script and do not use the `$` prefix.
codeExample:
<?php
define("TAX_RATE", 0.08);
const DISCOUNT = 0.1;
$price = 200;
$final = $price + ($price * TAX_RATE) - ($price * DISCOUNT);
echo "Final price: $" . $final;
?>

quiz.question: Can a constant be changed after definition?
